title: Cyathodes oxycedrus
intro: Cyathodes oxycedrus, also known as Kanahele's cyathodes, is a flowering plant species native to Hawaii. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cyathodes oxycedrus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Cyathodes oxycedrus is often used to make topical preparations, such as salves and ointments, as well as teas, to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Cyathodes oxycedrus is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of New Caledonia. This plant is a type of shrub that grows in the wild, producing small, star-shaped flowers. The classification of Cyathodes oxycedrus includes its order as Asterales and its family as Gentianaceae. Further classification includes its genus as Cyathodes and its species as C. oxycedrus.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Additionally, it may have antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating infections and wounds.
